title: Using revert(0x00, 0x04) instead of revert(0x1c, 0x04) for 4-byte custom errors
trigger_patterns: revert with 4 byte custom error selector
explanation: mstore(0x00, value) writes a full 32-byte word at offset 0x00. A 4-byte selector like 0xf4d678b8 is stored right-aligned, meaning it occupies bytes 28-31 (offsets 0x1c-0x1f). revert(0x1c, 0x04) correctly reads exactly those 4 bytes. revert(0x00, 0x04) reads bytes 0-3 which are 0x00000000 — returning an empty or zeroed selector.
why_wrong: revert(0x00, 0x04) returns the first 4 bytes of the 32-byte word which are all zeros when a 4-byte selector is stored. The ABI decoder receives 0x00000000 instead of the actual error selector, making the error undecodable in explorers and client code.
gas_impact: Zero gas difference — this is a correctness bug not a gas bug
